meta.name: Assign cell-type labels (CellTypist)
meta.description: Predict a cell-type label for the clusters with a pre-trained CellTypist model, voting within each Leiden cluster so every cluster gets a consistent cell-type name in obs[cell_type_key].
meta.provenance: CellTypist (Dominguez Conde et al., Science 2022). Step 3 of the Xenium region-analysis workflow.

readme: Step 3 of the workflow (run 'Cluster (Leiden) & top marker genes' first). Runs CellTypist with majority voting over the Leiden clusters (`cluster_key` used as the over-clustering), so the per-cell predictions are consolidated into one cell-type label per cluster, written to `obs[cell_type_key]` (plus a `<cell_type_key>_conf` confidence column). Pick a `model` matching your tissue — see https://www.celltypist.org/models (e.g. `Human_Lung_Atlas.pkl` for lung, `Immune_All_Low.pkl` for immune). The first use of a model downloads it (needs network). Follow with 'Neighborhood analysis (cellular neighborhoods)'.

params.name: model
params.tooltip: pre-trained CellTypist model (see celltypist.org/models); match your tissue

params.name: cluster_key
params.tooltip: cluster column to vote within, so each cluster gets one cell-type label

params.name: cell_type_key
params.tooltip: obs column to write the predicted cell-type labels into
